rulings.Case Title: SHU CHUN WANG VS. MERITOUCH, A GENERAL PARTNERSHIP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Creditor'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 18, 2015, Line 2, CREDITOR SHU WANG'S Motion To Amend Judgment. Granted. The Labor Commissioner Award explicitly states that Plaintiff was employed by Shu Sheng Wang and Xue Li Yang, each individually AND as general partners of Meritouch, a general partnership. (Declaration of HaoHao Song, Ex. 1, Labor Commissioner Award, p. 2.) Accordingly, Wang and Yang are not individually liable solely because they are general partners in Meritouch. Rather, the Labor Commissioner found that Wang and Yang also employed Plaintiff in their capacity as individuals. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: WAN LING SONG VS. TOMMY ZHONG YANG FANG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Tommy Fang, And Go Lorrie'S Airport Shuttle'S Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Plaintiff'S Disclosed Expert Witnesses
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday June 18, 2015, Line 6, Defendants Tommy Fang, And Go Lorrie's Airport Shuttle's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Number Of Plaintiff's Disclosed Expert Witnesses. Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is granted. Plaintiff shall specify 5 non-retained experts it intends to call at trial, and, if plaintiff must call more than those designated 5 non-retained expert witnesses, it must specify an additional 5 non-retained experts and must bear all costs related to depositions of those additional 5 expert witnesses, including the expert's fee to testify and the reporting costs (not attorneys fees). This order is without prejudice to any relief that may be requested of the trial judge at the time of trial. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: RASH B GHOSH VS. PAUL STEINER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Paul Steiners Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Document Production, Have Matters In Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 18, 2015, Line 6, DEFENDANT PAUL STEINER'S Motion To Compel Discovery Responses, Document Production, Have Matters In Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion to compel is granted. Plaintiff shall provide responses without objections, except as to privileged items, on or before July 1, 2015. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions to Defendant in the amount of $540 within 30 days of entry of this order. The request to deem requests admitted is denied without prejudice. The Notice of Motion does not state that Defendant was seeking to have requests for admission deemed admitted. The Court will not provide a court reporter for this discovery hearing. If any party wishes a reporter, the parties must meet and confer and agree on a single reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SHUN LI VS. DAMIEN QUINN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Boersch Shapiro Llp'S Motion To Withdraw As Counsel (Control Date)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 18, 2015, Line 7, Boersch Shapiro LLP'S Motion To Withdraw As Counsel. Denied without prejudice. Although no opposition was filed, the motion does not comply with CRC 3.1362. CRC 3.1362(a) provides that a motion to be relieved as counsel MUST be made on Judicial Council form MC-051. Additionally, CRC 3.1362(e) provides that a proposed order MUST be prepared on Judicial Council form MC-053 and MUST be lodged with the court with the moving papers. Moving party failed to comply with these requirements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT WERNER VS. LYFT, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs First Amended Complaint By Defendants Zimride, Inc And Lyft, Inc
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, June 18, 2015, Line 9, DEFENDANTS LYFT, INC., AND ZIMRIDE, INC.'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Granted with 10 days leave to amend. The First Amended Complaint does not adequately plead facts establishing that Defendants acted with oppression, fraud, or malice, as required by Civ. Code sec. 3294. Plaintiff cites Grimshaw v. Ford Motor Co. (1981) 119 Cal.App.3d 757, for the proposition that punitive damages are available in product liability cases. However, in Grimshaw, the Court found punitive damages were appropriate because Defendant Ford Motor Co.'s management had actual knowledge of the dangerous condition, but failed to take any remedial action. (Id. at 815.) Here, in contrast, there are no allegations that Defendants' management knew or should have known that the application was dangerous. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts, if he can in good faith, that Defendants knew or should have known that an application designed to be used while driving presents a dangerous condition because it necessarily requires a driver to look away from the road. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
